The author uses the bus example to explain how a sudden stop causes you to hit the inside of the bus. That's an analogy for how the brain moves inside the skull during a hit, leading to a concussion. The analogy is meant to help the reader understand the mechanics of a concussion. So, the purpose of the bus example is to explain concussions better. Looking at the options: A. C. Give a better explanation of concussions – This seems right because the analogy is used to explain the mechanism of how a concussion happens in a relatable way. D. Remind people to wear helmets while playing sports – The helmet advice comes later in the passage, but the bus example is earlier, focusing on the cause of concussions rather than the solution of wearing helmets.
